fab_section_md:
- The Hand's schemes produced the Triarchy alliance; the High Council of the Three Daughters accepted his offer at Tyrosh, sending ninety warships to the Gullet (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:17)
- He later emerged from Maegor's Holdfast with Alicent when King's Landing fell, and was the first traitor Rhaenyra beheaded, having served three kings as Hand (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:243)
- See above.
identity_line: Otto Hightower — The Hand's schemes produced the Triarchy alliance; the High Council of the Three Daughters accepted his offer at Tyrosh, sending ninety warships to the Gullet.

fab_section_md: - Though he lived, all joy left him and he blamed himself for abandoning his little brother Viserys (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:49)
identity_line: Aegon the Younger — Though he lived, all joy left him and he blamed himself for abandoning his little brother Viserys.

fab_section_md:
- The Lysene admiral of the Triarchy fleet took Viserys as his prize and divided his fleet to attack the Gullet north and south of Dragonstone (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:23)
- Of his ninety warships only twenty-eight limped home, and the widows of Myr and Tyrosh accused him of holding his own fleet back (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:43)
identity_line: Sharako Lohar — The Lysene admiral of the Triarchy fleet took Viserys as his prize and divided his fleet to attack the Gullet north and south of Dragonstone.

fab_section_md:
- Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne, Rhaenyra's son, he swept down on the Lysene galleys aboard Vermax (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:27)
- Several differing tales were told of how his dragon fell; when Vermax went down, Jace leapt free onto wreckage until crossbowmen killed him (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:41)
- His loss was felt more deeply than any other in the battle (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:47)
identity_line: Jacaerys Velaryon — Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne, Rhaenyra's son, he swept down on the Lysene galleys aboard Vermax.

fab_section_md:
- Jacaerys Velaryon's dragon at the Gullet. According to one account a crossbowman put an iron bolt through his eye, a version thought suspiciously like the death of Meraxes; another says a Myrish sailor's grapnel tore open his belly (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:37)
- He went down smoking and screaming and sank with a burning galley (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:39)
identity_line: Vermax — Jacaerys Velaryon's dragon at the Gullet. According to one account a crossbowman put an iron bolt through his eye, a version thought suspiciously like the death of Meraxes; another says a Myrish sailor's grapnel tore open his belly.

fab_section_md:
- Caught between two hosts on the banks of the Honeywine, his lines were crumbling until Daeron and Tessarion arrived (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:57)
- He feasted Daeron and knighted him, then enforced submissions across the Reach (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:215)
identity_line: Ormund Hightower — Caught between two hosts on the banks of the Honeywine, his lines were crumbling until Daeron and Tessarion arrived.

fab_section_md:
- The Hand and Kingmaker marched from King's Landing and reached an abandoned Harrenhal a day before Aemond (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:169)
- He counseled withdrawal south to join Ormund Hightower, judging Vhagar alone no match for six dragons (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:223)
- When Aemond refused, they parted ways and Cole led the host south (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:231)
identity_line: Criston Cole — The Hand and Kingmaker marched from King's Landing and reached an abandoned Harrenhal a day before Aemond.

fab_section_md:
- Aegon II's golden dragon, his torn wing left him unable to fly, yet he survived Walys Mooton's attack, slaying threescore (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:107)
- Afterward he vanished with no tracks, having taken wing again (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:109)
identity_line: Sunfyre — Aegon II's golden dragon, his torn wing left him unable to fly, yet he survived Walys Mooton's attack, slaying threescore.

fab_section_md: - He was among the slain, his body found a fortnight later by his brother Manfryd as charred flesh in melted armor (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:109)

- The Red Kraken of Pyke, courted by both claimants, made his choice and fell upon the Lannister shores (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:97)
- Unable to breach Casterly Rock once Johanna Lannister barred the gates, his ironmen sacked Lannisport and carried off more than six hundred women and girls (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:99)
identity_line: Dalton Greyjoy — The Red Kraken of Pyke, courted by both claimants, made his choice and fell upon the Lannister shores.

fab_section_md:
- The commander of the gold cloaks killed Alicent's brother Gwayne when he denounced him as a turncloak, then opened the gates (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:127)
- He was ennobled by Rhaenyra afterward (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:237)
identity_line: Luthor Largent — The commander of the gold cloaks killed Alicent's brother Gwayne when he denounced him as a turncloak, then opened the gates.

fab_section_md:
- The castellan yielded Harrenhal to Daemon, and Aemond took the swift surrender as proof of treason (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:175)
- Given a sword for trial by combat, he was cut to pieces and fed to Vhagar (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:181)
identity_line: Simon Strong — The castellan yielded Harrenhal to Daemon, and Aemond took the swift surrender as proof of treason.

fab_section_md: - Mushroom says she used love potions on Aemond and Criston, Munkun credits Aemond's respect for the older man, and Septon Eustace says Aemond alone was besotted with her (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:227)
identity_line: Alys Rivers — Mushroom says she used love potions on Aemond and Criston, Munkun credits Aemond's respect for the older man, and Septon Eustace says Aemond alone was besotted with her.

fab_section_md: - Caught between two armies at the Gods Eye, he dug in and was slain at the Fishfeed (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:209)
identity_line: Humfrey Lefford — Caught between two armies at the Gods Eye, he dug in and was slain at the Fishfeed.

fab_section_md: - The eleven-year-old new Lord of Raventree brought fresh levies to the Fishfeed and wept at the heaps of the dead (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:209)
identity_line: Benjicot Blackwood — The eleven-year-old new Lord of Raventree brought fresh levies to the Fishfeed and wept at the heaps of the dead.

fab_section_md:
- A grizzled squire who dealt Jason Lannister his mortal wound, afterward knighted by Lord Piper as "Longleaf the Lionslayer" (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:93)
- He later led survivors alongside the lords who joined the Fishfeed (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:191)
identity_line: Pate of Longleaf — A grizzled squire who dealt Jason Lannister his mortal wound, afterward knighted by Lord Piper as "Longleaf the Lionslayer".

fab_section_md: - Took command of the western host and slew Tristan Vance by swimming the Red Fork to take his lines from the rear (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:93)
identity_line: Adrian Tarbeck — Took command of the western host and slew Tristan Vance by swimming the Red Fork to take his lines from the rear.

fab_section_md: - Alicent's brother and second in command of the gold cloaks, he rushed to sound the warning but was seized and killed by Luthor Largent (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:127)
identity_line: Gwayne Hightower — Alicent's brother and second in command of the gold cloaks, he rushed to sound the warning but was seized and killed by Luthor Largent.

fab_section_md:
- Lately Addam of Hull, rider of Seasmoke, who sought out the Sea Snake after the Gullet (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:53)
- At King's Landing he remained aloft on Seasmoke as a warning against defiance (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:133)
identity_line: Addam Velaryon — Lately Addam of Hull, rider of Seasmoke, who sought out the Sea Snake after the Gullet.

fab_section_md: - Grand Maester who urged Aemond to seek Baratheon aid, then was seized by gold cloaks and taken to the black cells when he tried to summon Alicent's loyal lords (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p02:125)
identity_line: Orwyle — Grand Maester who urged Aemond to seek Baratheon aid, then was seized by gold cloaks and taken to the black cells when he tried to summon Alicent's loyal lords.
